Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

(2 comments)

I don't think the fmode change is fatal, but shouldn't be forgotten.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1
> 
> (2 comments)
> 
> I don't think the fmode change is fatal, but shouldn't be forgotten.

Yes, those changes will follow this patch. Since this is rebased on to top of existing patch, was picking up patch with minimal changes.

one just adds the enum for the flags, should be safe - [done]
one that changes only och_flags to use MDS_* flags - [done]
one that changes only fd_open_mode - TODO
one that removes O_LOV_DELAY_CREATE_18 and adds ll_*lov_delay_create - TODO
one that does mds-side cleanups - TODO
one that does most variable renaming (also prefer "lfd" over "fd" for ll_file_data, to avoid confusion with kernel fd) - [Done]

Patch Set 1:

Ping for review (Not Andreas)

parents.subject: LU-10499 pcc: keep mtime unchange when attach file into PCC

commit.subject: LU-12885 mds: add enums for MDS_OPEN flags (2/4)
commit.message:
LU-12885 mds: add enums for MDS_OPEN flags (2/4)

This patch is second of the series of patch that separates
kernel open flags from MDS open flags

This second step changes och_flags to use MDS_* flags. This
also renames 'struct ll_file_data' variable from 'fd' to 'lfd'.
As 'fd' is generally used to denote kernel fd.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Signed-off-by: Arshad Hussain <arshad.hussain@aeoncomputing.com>
Change-Id: I3b105da5930fde51a57b2cda7aef38cd1e1d774d
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55066
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
